phrasal_verbs.meaning: draw a line through sth written
try to find information in a book, etc
say sth out loud which you are reading
turn sth so the other side is towards you
write information on a piece of paper

A.title: Topic vocabulary (gap fill)
A.instructions: Complete using the correct form of the words and phrases given.

questions.sentence: Maria studied hard and hopes she will ...... (achieve / fail / pass) the exam.
If you don't revise, you might ...... (achieve / fail / pass) the test.
Our class has ...... (achieve / fail / pass) excellent results this term.
Please read the ...... (degree / experience / instruction) before you start the activity.
Working in a school was a useful ...... (degree / experience / instruction) for Liam.
You need a university ...... (degree / experience / instruction) for this job.
Typing quickly is an important ...... (course / qualification / skill).
I'm doing an online English ...... (course / qualification / skill).
This certificate is a useful ...... (course / qualification / skill) for teachers.
You have ...... (make progress / make sure / take an exam) a lot of ...... (make progress / make sure / take an exam) since September.
I'm going to ...... (make progress / make sure / take an exam) in biology next week.
Please ...... (make progress / make sure / take an exam) your name is on the paper.
I **wonder / search** if we'll get our marks today.
The new school **term / mark** starts in September.
Can you **remind / revise** me to bring my dictionary tomorrow?
Ask Eva for help. She's a science **expert / brain**.
Daniel is a **talented / smart** artist and his drawings are amazing.
Amy is very **clever / mental** and learns things quickly.
I didn't know the answer, so I **guessed / hesitated**.
Have you ever **considered / concentrated** studying abroad?
We wrote a **report / subject** about our history project.

C.title: Crossword
C.instructions: Complete the crossword. All the answers are words in bold in exercise B.

across.clue: You should ...... your notes before the test. (6)
The room was noisy, so I couldn't ...... . (11)

down.clue: Chess is good exercise for your ...... abilities. (6)
I got a good ...... in my geography test. (4)
I need to ...... for information about this topic. (6)
He ...... before answering the difficult question. (9)

D.title: Phrasal verbs (diary entry)
D.instructions: Write one word in each gap.
D.passage: We had a history test today. First, Mr Brown told us to turn our papers (1) ...... and write our names. Then he read (2) ...... the questions so everyone could hear them. We couldn't look (3) ...... any dates in our books, of course. I made a mistake in the first answer, so I rubbed it (4) ...... and wrote it again. Later, I crossed (5) ...... a whole sentence because it was wrong. I nearly ripped (6) ...... my paper because I was so nervous! Before the test ended, I wrote (7) ...... three important names I had remembered. At the end, Mr Brown pointed (8) ...... that we should check our spelling.

E.title: Prepositional phrases
E.instructions: Each of the words in bold is in the wrong sentence. Write the correct word.

questions.sentence: I learnt all the irregular verbs by **fact**. ......
Are you in **instance** of more homework at weekends? ......
The lesson looked difficult, but in **heart** it was quite simple. ......
Some subjects, for **conclusion** history, require a lot of reading. ......
In **favour**, students at this school work very hard. ......
You can finish your essay with the phrase 'In **general**'. ......

F.title: Word formation
F.instructions: Complete by changing the form of the word in capitals.

questions.sentence: Good ...... is important for every child. **EDUCATE**
This course is easy because it's for ...... . **BEGIN**
The firefighter showed great ...... during the rescue. **BRAVE**
You can find more information in the ...... section. **REFER**
The teacher asked for complete ...... during the test. **SILENT**
Our swimming ...... is very patient with beginners. **INSTRUCT**
That answer is ...... . Please try again. **CORRECT**
We learnt long ...... in maths today. **DIVIDE**
Can you ...... these instructions for younger students? **SIMPLE**
We have to ...... a poem for Friday. **MEMORY**

G.title: Word patterns
G.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

questions.sentence: Nobody should cheat ...... a test.
We're learning ...... the human brain in science.
What's your opinion ...... online lessons?
Don't confuse Austria ...... Australia.
The teacher asked me a question ...... the story.
I can't cope ...... all these exams at once.

H.title: Word patterns (sentence transformation)
H.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.

questions.sentence:
Mia is very good at drawing portraits.
**TALENTED** Mia is really ...... portraits.

The students were tired but didn't stop working.
**CONTINUED** The students were tired but they ...... their work.

I have no information about this subject.
**KNOW** I ...... this subject.

Lucy couldn't understand the exercise, so Tom helped her.
**HELPING** Tom was ...... the exercise.

Nobody can finish this project in ten minutes.
**CAPABLE** Nobody ...... this project in ten minutes.

I hope you manage to pass the exam.
**SUCCEED** I hope you ...... the exam.
